content_hints.description: Trollskull Manor is a four stories tall, abandoned building in Trollskull Alley that boasts balconies, a turret, and five chimneys. The former tavern is haunted by the poltergeist of its previous barkeeper, Lif, who causes mischief by smashing plates and breaking beer barrels.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see Trollskull Manor, a four-story structure boasting balconies, a turret, and five chimneys, one of the grandest in Trollskull Alley. The tavern's taproom is filled with broken furniture and tarnished silverware, and the air carries the scent of old ale and decay.

npcs.name: Lif
npcs.role: the poltergeist (specter) of the tavern’s previous barkeeper

renovation_options.name: Basic Repairs
renovation_options.effect: Tavern becomes functional. Lif stops breaking things.

renovation_options.name: Full Renovation
renovation_options.effect: Tavern becomes profitable. Generates 10 gp/tenday income.

renovation_options.name: Guild License
renovation_options.effect: Legal permission to operate. Required by Waterdeep law.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Manor is a multistory structure, with each level standing 20 feet higher than the one below it. Its rooms feature 15-foot-high ceilings and 7-foot-high doorways.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the kitchen hearth is cold and cavernous, large enough to stand in without stooping. Copper pots hang from ceiling hooks, their surfaces tarnished to a deep green.

renovation_options.name: Restock Kitchen
renovation_options.effect: Basic cooking supplies. Can prepare simple meals.

renovation_options.name: Hire a Cook
renovation_options.effect: Per tenday. A competent cook improves tavern reputation.

npcs.name: Kalain
npcs.role: unable to behave politely before strangers, for she believes they’re all assassins sent by Dagult Neverember to murder her.

npcs.name: VhasparHolmdreg
npcs.role: LG male Illuskan human priest, who rents a room in the west wing of the residence.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Manor is a multistory structure, with each level standing 20 feet higher than the one below it. Its rooms have 15-foot-high ceilings, with 7-foot-high doorways connecting them.
content_hints.read_aloud: You descend creaking steps into a cellar of damp stone and packed earth. Empty wine racks line the walls, their bottles long gone, and the air is cold enough to make your breath mist.
content_hints.investigation_success: Behind a false section of wine rack: a hidden compartment containing Lif's personal ledger and a pouch of 50 gold pieces -- his life savings, hidden before his murder.

loot.name: Lif's Ledger
loot.description: The former owner's accounting book. Details the manor's debts and the name of his killer: 'Turned away by the Guild. They sent a man with a dead smile. I write this knowing I will not survive the night.'

npcs.name: Kalain
npcs.role: unable to behave politely before strangers, for she believes they’re all assassins sent by Dagult Neverember to murder her.

npcs.name: VhasparHolmdreg
npcs.role: LG male Illuskan human priest, who rents a room in the west wing of the residence.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Manor is a multistory structure, with each level standing 20 feet higher than the one below it. Its rooms feature 15-foot-high ceilings and 7-foot-high doorways.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a narrow hallway running past four small guest rooms, each door standing ajar. Inside, straw mattresses sag on iron frames, and washstands hold empty pitchers.

renovation_options.name: Furnish Guest Rooms
renovation_options.effect: Comfortable rooms. Can rent to guests for 5 sp/night each.

npcs.name: Kalain
npcs.role: unable to behave politely before strangers, for she believes they’re all assassins sent by Dagult Neverember to murder her.

npcs.name: VhasparHolmdreg
npcs.role: LG male Illuskan human priest, who rents a room in the west wing of the residence.

content_hints.description: Trollskull Manor is a four stories tall, abandoned building that boasts balconies, a turret, and five chimneys, making it one of the grandest in Trollskull Alley. Characters can refurnish, rebuild, rename, and otherwise personalize their new stronghold to their hearts’ content.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the grand scale of Trollskull Manor, four stories tall and boasting balconies, a turret, and five chimneys. As you explore, you notice this abandoned building is one of the grandest in Trollskull Alley.

npcs.name: Kalain
npcs.role: unable to behave politely before strangers, for she believes they’re all assassins sent by Dagult Neverember to murder her.

npcs.name: VhasparHolmdreg
npcs.role: LG male Illuskan human priest, who rents a room in the west wing of the residence.

content_hints.description: Four stories tall and boasting balconies, a turret, and five chimneys, the abandoned building is one of the grandest in Trollskull Alley. Characters can refurnish, rebuild, rename, and otherwise personalize their new stronghold to their hearts’ content.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a four-story, abandoned building boasting balconies, a turret, and five chimneys, one of the grandest in Trollskull Alley. As you enter, you can sense the history of this grand manor, though the tavern rooms within are filled with the broken furniture and tarnished silverware of a long-forgotten establishment.

npcs.name: Fala Lefaliir
npcs.dialogue: Welcome to the alley! I run Corellon's Crown -- the herbalist shop next door. If you need poultices, tinctures, or someone to complain to about the noise, I'm your elf.
npcs.notes: Wood elf herbalist. Non-binary. Knows all the alley's gossip. Can supply healing potions at cost (40 gp) as a favor to good neighbors.

npcs.name: Vincent Trench
npcs.dialogue: Trench. Private investigator. If you need someone found or something proven, my rates are reasonable. If you need something un-found or un-proven, my rates are less reasonable.
npcs.notes: Actually a disguised rakshasa named Valanthar. Runs a legitimate detective agency as cover. Not hostile unless his true nature is discovered.

content_hints.exit_note: Leads to Trollskull Alley and the North Ward of Waterdeep.
